claim: The recipes section (25 folios, 11,611 words, entropy 3.8586 bits) and the herbal section (129 folios, 10,872 words, entropy 3.8478 bits) share a common cipher and possibly the same underlying language, while the biological section (19 folios, 6,315 words, entropy 3.7977 bits) uses a distinct encoding, as evidenced by its lower entropy and different word-length distribution.
evidence_for: Recipes and herbal sections have nearly identical entropy (3.8586 vs. 3.8478 bits), suggesting the same encoding regime
Biological section entropy (3.7977) is detectably lower and its folio count is small enough that vocabulary overlap can be tested
Thematic similarity between herbal and recipes (both botanical/practical) supports shared authorship and cipher
evidence_against: Entropy differences across sections are small (range 0.186 bits), potentially within noise for section sizes of a few thousand words
Currier A/B split crosscuts section boundaries, so section-level encoding differences may reflect A/B differences rather than section-specific ciphers
Biological section's lower entropy could simply reflect a more constrained vocabulary (anatomical terms) rather than a different cipher
test: Compute pairwise cosine similarity of section-level glyph unigram and bigram frequency vectors for all seven sections. Cluster sections by similarity. Test whether recipes and herbal cluster together and biological clusters separately using Ward linkage hierarchical clustering.
test_metric: Dendrogram structure from hierarchical clustering on bigram frequency vectors; silhouette score for a 2-cluster partition (herbal+recipes vs. others); chi-squared test on word-length distributions between herbal and biological.
test_threshold: Confirmation: herbal and recipes share a cluster with silhouette score > 0.4, and biological word-length chi-squared p < 0.01 vs. herbal. Denial: silhouette score < 0.2 or biological clusters with herbal at p > 0.10.

reasoning: Identifying cipher homogeneity across sections allows decipherment attempts to pool token counts from matching sections, increasing statistical power for frequency analysis.
